mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market acts as a leading indicator of impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and residential construction permits signals synchronized demand recovery across business and household sectors, driving sustained equity outperformance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% but housing starts remain below trend, or cross-validation IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient corporate capacity utilization and pricing power, supporting equity valuations despite higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises alongside FEDFUNDS increases exceeding 50 basis points per quarter, indicating supply-driven inflation without demand support.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real consumer spending directly translates to higher corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the immediate one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains statistically indistinguishable from zero over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant difference between positive and negative RSAFS growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating core inflation erodes real yields and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than markets price in, compressing equity multiples and depressing forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, reducing wage-price spiral fears and supporting equity risk premiums over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts negative returns when PAYEMS rises > 100k and UNRATE falls, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding historical averages during a credit expansion phase signal strong household balance sheet health, driving immediate positive equity returns as consumer discretionary earnings outlook improves.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when HOUST exceeds 1400 but GDPC1 growth decelerates below 1.0%, or the trigger fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over 3 consecutive years.
